subject: 0.7.1-alpha real acceptance and next-stage unified governance plan

local_evidence.fact: main is 4877ecb and the working tree was clean during this audit.

0.7.0-alpha host and dry-run passed; one normal dispatch was blocked before job creation by legacy receipt authority; a CodeBuddy canary worker itself returned exit code 0 and the required marker but did not write readiness after concurrent main-checkout drift.
The current Desktop process loads 0.7.1-alpha from the Codex-managed personal plugin cache with runtime contract SHA256 8abc88e40a463839b1b340121bf7e3955141138f9e3f7360600edeac71a9f3f5.

The installed plugin, cache and marketplace are ready; provider_readiness is blocked because the current generation has no matching readiness tuple. Legacy receipt is diagnostic only. Maintenance and inventory are degraded, not the direct dispatch blocker.
The script compares the entire main checkout git status before and after a successful worker run, then aborts before writing readiness if the status differs.
isActiveStatus excludes completed/failed/blocked/skipped/cancelled but not process_exited, timed_out, watcher_failed or unknown, so exited jobs can be returned as active lanes.
v0.7.1-alpha is published with the setup zip and SHA256 sidecar. The zip asset digest is sha256:519028b8da926f53512638421dad56fd9ae50d6b7177ea64129da467fc4a35fb.
Release On Main for merge commit 4877ecb succeeded.
Dependabot TypeScript update CI failed at test-release-intent because it demanded a new release tag after the script had already classified the change as development-only.
Dependabot @types/node update failed for the same release-intent/tag-gating reason, before compatibility tests.

external_evidence.fact: The research route admitted official documentation, implementation evidence and GitHub issue evidence, and required an evidence register.
VS Code describes extension integration tests as running in a special Extension Development Host; it documents isolated user-data configurations for deterministic trust-state runs.
MCP Inspector documents connection, capability negotiation, tool schema/input/result testing, logs and edge-case testing.
Google SRE defines canarying as a partial, time-limited change evaluation and stresses attributable, isolated signals and explicit evaluation metrics.
GitHub documents attestation of built artifacts and verification with gh attestation verify.
Search result states installed plugins add skills, connectors and MCP tools to new chats. Direct body extraction returned HTTP 403, so this is treated as discovery-level confirmation only.
external_evidence.gap: Direct page extraction was blocked by HTTP 403; no stronger claim is made.

external_evidence.fact: Search result states local Codex clients can connect directly to MCP servers and share configuration for the same Codex host. Direct body extraction returned HTTP 403.
external_evidence.gap: Direct page extraction was blocked by HTTP 403; no stronger claim is made.

research_limitations: OpenAI Codex manual helper and direct OpenAI page extraction both encountered HTTP 403 in this environment; the report uses only their search snippets for narrow discovery claims.

A clean-worktree acceptance ran one real dispatch attempt for 0.7.1-alpha. It was blocked before worker creation because the current generation has no matching readiness tuple; no readiness or receipt was edited during the read-only acceptance.
